name: Petition to Late-Add or Late-Drop a Course
description: Call an academic advisor, dean's office, or registrar to add or drop a course past the published deadline — by petition, instructor consent, or dean's signature.
disclaimer: Late-add and late-drop policies vary widely; some schools require an instructor signature only, others require a college-level committee. Financial-aid impact (full-time vs. part-time) and tuition refund schedules are separate from academic petitions — handle both.

context.when_to_use: The published add/drop deadline has passed and the user needs to (a) add a course they were attending but never officially registered, (b) drop a course that's tanking their GPA before final grades, (c) swap a section due to a schedule conflict that emerged after the deadline, or (d) replace a class that turned out to be misadvised (wrong level, missing prerequisite, taught very differently than catalog).
preconditions: User can articulate WHY the deadline was missed in one sentence (illness, work change, advising error, family emergency, instructor unresponsive).
User has the course CRN / section number, instructor name, and current grade/standing if available.
User knows the financial implications: tuition refund window, financial-aid full-time threshold (usually 12 credits undergrad), insurance / housing thresholds.
User has checked the school's petition form — most late-add/drop petitions require a written petition AND a signature from the instructor, advisor, and sometimes a dean.

principles: Late petitions live on documented circumstances, not on regret. 'I should have dropped earlier' is weak; 'I was hospitalized March 3–8, here's the discharge summary' is strong.
The earlier you petition past the deadline, the easier the approval. Two weeks late is a paperwork exercise; ten weeks late is a battle.
Instructor support is the single highest-leverage signature. If the instructor backs the petition, advisors and deans almost always defer.
There are usually TWO deadlines: add/drop (administrative) and withdraw (a W on the transcript). Past add/drop, the realistic ask is often 'late withdrawal' not 'late drop' — know the difference.
Financial-aid full-time status is a cliff, not a slope. Dropping from 12 to 9 credits mid-term can trigger Pell/Stafford recalculation. Loop the financial-aid office BEFORE confirming a drop.
Dean's offices grant exceptions for what registrars can't. If front-line advising says no, the assistant dean for student academic affairs is the real target.

phrases.opener_late_add: Hi, I'm in week [N] of [course] but I never officially added it — I thought I was registered. I'd like to start a late-add petition. Can you tell me what your office needs from me, and who has signature authority?
phrases.opener_late_drop: Hi, I'd like to petition for a late drop / late withdrawal from [course]. The standard deadline was [date]. The reason is [one-line cause]. What's the process and what documentation do you require?
phrases.ask_instructor_first: Before I file the petition — does it make sense for me to talk to the instructor first to confirm they'll sign? My understanding is that an instructor-supported petition has a better chance.
phrases.ask_about_W: Past the withdrawal deadline, what are my options? A W on the transcript is fine; an F is the outcome I'm trying to avoid.
phrases.documented_cause: I have [doctor's note / death certificate / employer letter / police report] for [dates]. Should I submit it with the petition or attach it later?
phrases.ask_aid_impact: If I drop this course, my credit load goes from [X] to [Y]. Could you check whether that drops me below full-time for financial-aid purposes, and if so, what the recalculation would look like?
phrases.ask_for_dean: If the registrar's office can't approve this at the front-line level, could the petition be routed to the assistant dean for student affairs / academic standards? I want to make sure it gets a substantive review.
phrases.graceful_close_success: Thank you. To confirm: I'm submitting [form] with [signatures and documents] by [date]. You'll route it to [committee/dean], and I can expect a decision by [date]. Is there an email I can use to check status?
phrases.graceful_close_failure: Okay. Could you put in writing what specifically would have made the petition successful, and whether I can re-petition in a future term? And is there a path to grade replacement if I retake the course later?

tactics.name: Lock in instructor support first
tactics.script: Use `ask_instructor_first` framing during the call, but the actual ask is to the instructor by email/office hours: 'Will you sign / email support for my late-add or late-drop petition for [reason]?' Get a yes in writing before filing.

tactics.name: Distinguish drop vs. withdrawal
tactics.when: The user says 'I want to drop it' past the drop deadline.
tactics.script: Use `ask_about_W`. A 'drop' removes the course from the transcript (and refunds tuition by schedule). A 'withdrawal' leaves a W on the transcript with no GPA impact. Past the drop deadline, the realistic ask is usually W. Make sure the user understands which they're requesting.

tactics.name: Document the cause specifically
tactics.when: Petition asks 'why now and not by the deadline?'
tactics.script: Use `documented_cause`. Dates on documents must straddle or precede the deadline you missed. A doctor's note dated AFTER the deadline still works if symptoms began before. Get explicit dates.

tactics.name: Pre-clear financial aid
tactics.when: Drop would cross a credit-load threshold.
tactics.script: Use `ask_aid_impact`. Call financial aid SEPARATELY before finalizing the drop — registrar won't model aid impact, and a 'Return to Title IV' calculation after the fact can owe the student thousands.

tactics.name: Escalate to the dean's office
tactics.when: Registrar or advising says no but the cause is documented and serious.
tactics.script: Use `ask_for_dean`. Dean-level review is standard for documented medical, mental-health, family-emergency, or advising-error cases. Specifically ask for 'assistant dean for student affairs' or 'academic standards committee' — not just 'the dean'.

tactics.name: Ask about retroactive add/drop windows
tactics.when: Issue is discovered in a later term.
tactics.script: Some schools allow retroactive petitions up to one academic year (or longer for medical). Don't assume the door is closed just because the term ended.

tactics.name: Ask about grade replacement / repeat policy as a fallback
tactics.when: Late-drop is denied and the user is heading toward a poor grade.
tactics.script: Many schools allow a course to be retaken with the new grade replacing or averaging with the old. This is a separate process from late-drop and may be the better path if the petition fails.

boundaries: Do NOT submit a late-add petition for a course the user did not actually attend — registrars cross-check attendance and falsifying it is academic dishonesty.
Do NOT fabricate documentation. Verification calls to doctors / employers happen and the consequences are academic misconduct, not just denial.
Do NOT drop a course without confirming financial-aid impact in writing.
Do NOT promise the instructor anything beyond what the operator has authorized (e.g. completing missed work).
Do NOT skip the petition form. Verbal yeses from a registrar don't post in the system.
success_signals: Petition number / case number is issued.
Instructor agrees to sign or send a supporting email.
Advisor confirms the petition will route to dean / committee, not be denied at front desk.
Financial-aid impact is modeled and is acceptable (or zero).
Decision turnaround is named (commonly 5–10 business days).
failure_signals: Instructor refuses to sign for a late-add.
Petition is past the school's outer deadline (commonly 1 year retroactive).
Drop would drop user below half-time, triggering immediate loan repayment.
Multiple prior petitions on file — schools tighten on repeat askers.
Course is required for an upcoming deadline (graduation, prerequisite for next term) and dropping creates a worse problem.

exit_strategy.on_success: Confirm: form submitted, signatures collected, decision date, who signs final, financial-aid status confirmed. Get the petition reference number.
exit_strategy.on_failure: Get the denial in writing with specific reasoning. Pivot to: incomplete grade (talk to instructor), grade replacement next term, medical withdrawal (different process), or appeal to dean if not yet tried.
follow_ups: Email the operator the petition number, decision date, and financial-aid impact memo.
If approved: verify the course status changes in the student-information system and on the transcript.
If denied: research grade-replacement / repeat policy and incomplete-grade option as next steps.

required_user_info: Reason for missing the deadline (one sentence)
Current credit load and whether drop crosses a threshold
Whether instructor has been contacted and their stance
Petition form (school's specific name) and any prior submissions
